subject: Cloud Computing
title: Azure's Government Cloud
description: A micro-lesson that makes Azure's Government Cloud usable.

hook.text: The U.S. Department of Defense wanted a $10 billion cloud contract. Microsoft won it — then lost it — then won again.

buildup.text: JEDI, the Pentagon's cloud deal, was designed as winner-take-all. Microsoft beat AWS in the final round.

discovery.text: Azure Government runs in isolated data centers with special security clearances required for access.

twist.text: AWS protested, claiming political bias. The contract was eventually canceled and replaced with a multi-cloud deal.

climax.text: Government cloud is a massive market. Azure's security certifications give it a strong advantage.

punchline.text: Government cloud contracts are worth billions. And lawsuits.

quiz.question: What happened to the JEDI cloud contract?
options: Microsoft won, AWS protested, and the contract was eventually canceled and restructured
AWS won the contract and completed it without issues
Google Cloud won and built the Pentagon's entire infrastructure
